authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(48) — Gualala River (Mendocino and Sonoma Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (48) Gualala River (Mendocino and Sonoma Cos.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. Main stem below the confluence of Wheatfield and South Forks. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. Fishing from a flotation device is prohibited from Nov. 15 through Feb. 28 from the confluence of the North Fork to the Highway 1 bridge. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00(b) — Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County Coastal Streams: Special Low Flow Conditions Closures
sources.quotedText: (b) Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County coastal streams: Stream Closures: Special Low Flow Conditions.

sources.title: Guide: Fishing Gualala River — California Fish Plants
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 253221, "Gualala River", Stream, Mendocino/Sonoma Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":253221,"gaz_name":"Gualala River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Sonoma"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13751732.2017, 4688569.1277) converts to lon -123.533912, lat 38.768527, at the river's mouth near the town of Gualala, replacing the file's previous coordinate (-123.4158, 38.7014), which was off by ~12.7 km.

groups.humanSummary: Gualala River main stem below the confluence of the Wheatfield and South Forks (Mendocino and Sonoma Cos.): open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, artificial lures with barbless hooks only through October 31 then barbless hooks only through March 31, flotation-device fishing banned Nov. 15 - Feb. 28 from the North Fork confluence to the Highway 1 bridge, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
groups.verbatimText: (48) Gualala River (Mendocino and Sonoma Cos.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. Main stem below the confluence of Wheatfield and South Forks. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. Fishing from a flotation device is prohibited from Nov. 15 through Feb. 28 from the confluence of the North Fork to the Highway 1 bridge. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.description: This water is also subject to 14 CCR §8.00(b), the Mendocino/Sonoma/Marin County coastal streams special low-flow closure provision, under which fishing may be closed when streamflow at a designated gauge falls below a prescribed threshold.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, on the main stem below the confluence of the Wheatfield and South Forks.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from November 1 through March 31; bait is allowed on barbless hooks.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Fishing from any flotation device (boat, raft, float tube, etc.) is prohibited from Nov. 15 through Feb. 28, on the stretch from the confluence of the North Fork downstream to the Highway 1 bridge.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing from a flotation device is prohibited from November 15 through February 28 from the North Fork confluence to the Highway 1 bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: Fishing from a flotation device is prohibited from Nov. 15 through Feb. 28 from the confluence of the North Fork to the Highway 1 bridge.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
